title: Memecylon fasciculare
taxonomy: Memecylon fasciculare is classified within the family Melastomataceae. This family comprises approximately 165 genera of flowering plants, commonly known as the melastome family. The genus Memecylon belongs to this family and contains around 160 species, primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Memecylon fasciculare is further divided into various species based on its morphology and distribution. More specifically, its classification is still being researched and refined by botanists and taxonomists.

This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
